name: EOS Worldwide
validation_questions: Confirm Item 19 cohort size and definition with franchisor.
Confirm current auditor and most recent audit opinion.

internal_unresolveds.reason: Rising attrition cause unknown — 49 voluntary departures in 2024 (up from 13 in 2022). FDD does not explain trend.
Cash flowing to affiliates despite declining cash balance ($233K). Collectability of $5.7M intercompany receivable uncertain.
Note purchase agreement terms (interest rate, maturity, covenants) not disclosed beyond balance and interest paid. Balance grew from ~$2.4M to ~$17.9M in 2 years.
Materially different terms on renewal — no specificity on potential changes.
Warm leads fee language ambiguous — 'initial (or any one-time) session' could be read narrowly or broadly.

Item 20 shows 3 terminations + 49 ceased ops in calendar year 2024. Item 19 notes 47 operational franchisees terminated during Feb 2024-Jan 2025. Different time periods/definitions create confusion ab
Member's deficit increases by $1 ($3,715,000→$3,716,000) between 2023 and 2024 with no apparent distribution.
